ddls:
CREATE TABLE "parts" (
  "part_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each part */,
  "part_no" TEXT /* Part number */,
  "thickness" REAL /* Thickness of the part */,
  "identifying_mark" INTEGER /* Identifying mark on the part */,
  "part_type" TEXT /* Type of part (e.g., engine, transmission, brake) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the part */,
  "unit_price" TEXT /* Price of the part */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier */,
  "reorder_level" INTEGER /* Minimum quantity for reordering */,
  "reorder_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity to reorder when level falls below minimum */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("part_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "warehouses" (
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each warehouse */,
  "warehouse_name" TEXT /* Name of the warehouse */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the warehouse */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum storage capacity of the warehouse */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the warehouse */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the warehouse contact person */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the warehouse contact person */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the warehouse contact person */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory entry */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the part in the warehouse */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the inventory was last updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the inventory */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who placed the order */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part ordered */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the part ordered */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered) */,
  "total_cost" TEXT /* Total cost of the order */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used for the order */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Shipping address for the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "part_compatibilities" (
  "compatibility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compatibility entry */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part */,
  "compatible_part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compatible part */,
  "compatibility_type" TEXT /* Type of compatibility (e.g., direct, indirect) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compatibility_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_part_compatibilities_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_part_compatibilities_compatible_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("compatible_part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, warehouse manager, sales representative) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "part_movements" (
  "movement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movement entry */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part */,
  "from_warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse the part was moved from */,
  "to_warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse the part was moved to */,
  "movement_date" TEXT /* Date the part was moved */,
  "movement_type" TEXT /* Type of movement (e.g., transfer, return) */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the movement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_part_movements_to_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("to_warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_part_movements_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_part_movements_from_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("from_warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "suppliers" (
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier */,
  "supplier_name" TEXT /* Name of the supplier */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the supplier */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the supplier */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the supplier */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_items" (
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order item */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the part in the order */,
  "unit_price" TEXT /* Unit price of the part in the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "invoices" (
  "invoice_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each invoice */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order */,
  "invoice_date" TEXT /* Date the invoice was generated */,
  "total_amount" TEXT /* Total amount of the invoice */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("invoice_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_invoices_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shipments" (
  "shipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shipment */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order */,
  "shipment_date" TEXT /* Date the order was shipped */,
  "carrier" TEXT /* Shipping carrier */,
  "tracking_number" TEXT /* Tracking number for the shipment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shipment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shipments_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)
